# NGSpeciesID-Pipeline-usage
How to use NGSpecies ID Pipeline for ONT data analysis

cd /data/conda-envs/NGSpeciesID
conda activate NGSpeciesID

#Running test
mkdir test_ngspeciesID
cd test_ngspeciesID
NGSpeciesID --help

#Download fastq files
curl -LO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ksahlin/NGSpeciesID/master/test/sample_h1.fastq

#Run NGSpeciesID
NGSpeciesID --ont --fastq sample_h1.fastq --outfolder ./sample_h1 --consensus --medaka

#Usage NGSpeciesID needs a fastq file generated by an Oxford Nanopore basecaller.
NGSpeciesID --ont --consensus --medaka --fastq [reads.fastq] --outfolder [/path/to/output] 

The argument --ont simply means --k 13 --w 20. These arguments can be set manually without the --ont flag. Specify number of cores with --t.

NGSpeciesID can also run with racon as polisher. For example

NGSpeciesID --ont --consensus --racon --racon_iter 3 --fastq [reads.fastq] --outfolder [/path/to/output]
cd .. 

#Running CMV Samples 
cd /M_Rutendo
mkdir
mkdir nameofdirectory
cd nameofdirectory
#Add all fastq files of amplicons...this is after basecalling and demultiplexing

#NGSpeciesID Step by step
It is assumed that the input for analysis will be basecalled AND classified fastq reads generated from basecalling in galaxy with Dorado or you can refer to the Dorado basecalling reporsitory
